---
title: "ORCA: Progress and Scope"
author: "Ankush Jain"
institute: "Mochi: 2025-03-05"
format: beamer
include-in-header:
  file: "header.tex"
#include-before-body: 
  #file: "header.tex"
#include-after-body: 
  #file: "header.tex"
keep-tex: true
---

## ORCA: Brief Overview

- ORCA: Observability with Realtime Control and Aggregation

- Original motivation: programmable observability
  - Think of a helpful view over telemetry
  - Express it as a SQL query
  - Overlay will stream just the relevant data in real-time

. . .

- Ingredients: on-fabric aggregation and real-time control
  - Interfaces to collect data as Arrow tables
  - An overlay to move it around
  - A distributed SQL engine to do telemetry aggregation
  - Consistent control semantics for online probing, tuning etc.

## ORCA: Overlay

![Overlay: Aggregation Path](../tikz-exps/pdf/overlay-agg-new.pdf)

## ORCA: Overlay Progress

- Implemented Mercury-based Overlay
  - Addresses overlay entities using `ovid_t`
  - `ovid_t`: 32-bit identifier, ~ rank for MPI ranks
  - Special `ovid_t` range for aggregators (unique for controller)
  - Special values for broadcast, dedicated routes etc.

<!-- ```{=latex} -->
<!-- \pause -->
<!-- ``` -->

. . .

- Bootstraps using a two-step process
  - Initial discovery using a TCP socket
  - Bootstrap protocol using Mercury
  - Can be extended for arbitrary topologies

```{=latex}
\pause
```

- Difference from `deltafs-shuffle`:
  - Purely an addressing and routing layer
  - No batching, intra-MPI routing, delivery, flow control
  - (Relegated to higher layers)

## ORCA: Other Components

- Defined MPI-side interfaces (data collection, hooks etc.)
- Implemented single-node SQL aggregation
  - Datafusion and Rust (like a single DuckDB instance)
- *Ongoing*: a two-phase commit for control messages
- *After*: distributed queries with shuffling and pushdown

```{=latex}
\pause
```

### Planned: Export Slices to OTF2

- Aggregated telemetry visualized in real-time
- Fine-grained elemetry stored on-disk (Parquet)
  - Conditional, filtered, timestep-partitioned

```{=latex}
\pause
```
- `generate-otf2 timesteps 200-220 ranks 512-1024`
  - Range query over timestep, fast with Arrow
  - Visualize using standard tracing GUIs
